chapter: ENG9_4 – In the Kingdom of Fools
title: In the Kingdom of Fools – Medium SAQs

questions.question: Why did the king change night into day and day into night?
questions.answer: The king wanted to rule differently from other kings, so he ordered people to work at night and sleep during the day.

questions.question: How did the people react to the king’s strange rule?
questions.answer: The people followed the rule out of fear because disobedience was punishable by death.

questions.question: Why were the guru and disciple surprised when they entered the kingdom?
questions.answer: They saw the entire town sleeping during the day, which was unusual and surprising.

questions.question: What made the guru realise that it was a kingdom of fools?
questions.answer: The strange rule of day and night and the equal price of all goods made him realise it.

questions.question: Why did the disciple refuse to leave the kingdom?
questions.answer: He stayed because everything was cheap and he could eat as much food as he wanted.

questions.question: What incident started the court case in the kingdom?
questions.answer: A thief died when the wall of a merchant’s house collapsed on him.

questions.question: Why did the thief’s brother complain to the king?
questions.answer: He believed the merchant was responsible for his brother’s death.

questions.question: Why did the merchant blame the bricklayer?
questions.answer: He said the bricklayer had built a weak wall.

questions.question: Why did the bricklayer blame the dancing girl?
questions.answer: He said her jingling anklets distracted him while building the wall.

questions.question: Why did the dancing girl blame the goldsmith?
questions.answer: The goldsmith delayed making her jewellery, forcing her to walk past the wall repeatedly.

questions.question: Why was the goldsmith blamed by the king?
questions.answer: Because the delay caused the chain of events leading to the thief’s death.

questions.question: Why was the merchant finally sentenced to death?
questions.answer: The king concluded that the merchant’s family was responsible for the weak wall.

questions.question: Why could the merchant not be executed?
questions.answer: He was too thin to fit the execution stake.

questions.question: How was the disciple caught by the king’s servants?
questions.answer: They searched the town for a fat man and found the disciple.

questions.question: What did the disciple remember when he was captured?
questions.answer: He remembered the guru’s warning that the kingdom was dangerous.

questions.question: How did the guru reach the kingdom again?
questions.answer: He came after seeing the disciple’s trouble through his magical powers.

questions.question: Why did the guru insist on dying first?
questions.answer: He wanted to trick the king and save his disciple.

questions.question: Why did the king postpone the execution?
questions.answer: He wanted time to think about the guru’s mysterious request.

questions.question: What did the guru secretly tell the king?
questions.answer: That whoever died first on the stake would be reborn as king.

questions.question: Why did the king decide to die first?
questions.answer: He wanted to become king again in the next life.

questions.question: What happened to the king and the minister at night?
questions.answer: They secretly replaced the guru and disciple and were executed.

questions.question: Why did the people panic after the execution?
questions.answer: They discovered that their king and minister were dead.

questions.question: Why did the people request the guru to become their king?
questions.answer: Because they wanted a wise ruler to lead them.

questions.question: What condition did the guru set before ruling?
questions.answer: He said he would change all the old foolish laws.

questions.question: What major reform did the guru introduce?
questions.answer: He restored the normal cycle of day and night.
